claim_short: 1805.03292 Alice Barbara Tumpach Banach Poisson-Lie groups and Bruhat-Poisson structure of the restricted Grassmannian Remark 1.14 asks for existence and construction of a trace class operator whose triangular projection is not trace class. Full answer: finite-dimensional triangular projections on S_1^n have unbounded norms; choose blocks B_k with trace norm at most 2^{-k} and triangular-projection trace norm at...
notes: registry_status=full | review_focus=The key point for review is the finite-dimensional witness lemma: if the finite triangular projections on S_1^n were uniformly bounded, density of finite-rank operators would extend triangular truncation to a bounded operator on all of S_1(H), contradicting...
packet_title: Full Result: Trace-Class Operator with Non-Trace-Class Triangular Projection
source_question: Source paper: Alice Barbara Tumpach, *Banach Poisson-Lie groups and Bruhat-Poisson structure of the restricted Grassmannian*, arXiv:1805.03292. Location: page 9, Remark 1.14, after the definition of triangular truncations. Question: whether there exists a trace-class operator whose triangular projection is not trace class.
source_title: Banach Poisson-Lie groups and Bruhat-Poisson structure of the restricted Grassmannian
